questions.stem:
—______ you ______ your homework yet?
—Yes, I ______ it an hour ago.
questions.explanation: 正确答案是B。第一空根据yet（已经/还）这个标志词，可知问句要用现在完成时（Have...finished），表示过去动作对现在的影响；第二空根据an hour ago（一小时前）这个具体的过去时间状语，可知答句要用一般过去时（finished）。A选项第一空用Did，与yet搭配不当；C选项第二空用have finished，与ago矛盾；D选项第一空用Did，第二空用have finished，都不符合时间状语的要求。解题技巧：看到yet/ever/never/already/just用现在完成时；看到yesterday/ago/last week用一般过去时。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The boy ______ is wearing a red jacket is my cousin.
questions.explanation: 正确答案是A。先行词是The boy（指人），且关系代词在定语从句中作主语（is wearing的主语），所以用who。B选项which用于指物；C选项whom虽然指人，但只能作宾语，不能作主语；D选项whose表示'...的'，在从句中作定语，后面需要接名词。解题技巧：指人用who/whom/that，指物用which/that；看空后是否缺主语，缺主语用who/which，不缺主语可用whom/whose。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The book ______ by many students in our class last month.
questions.explanation: 正确答案是B。根据时间状语last month（上个月）可知用一般过去时；主语The book与read之间是被动关系（书被读），所以用一般过去时的被动语态was read。A选项is read是一般现在时的被动语态，时态不符；C选项reads是主动语态；D选项read是主动语态的过去式或原形。解题技巧：被动语态结构为be+done，先根据时间确定be的时态，再看主语单复数。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem:
—Is that Mr. Wang knocking at the door?
—It ______ be him. He has gone to Shanghai on business.
questions.explanation: 正确答案是C。根据后句'He has gone to Shanghai'（他去上海出差了，现在还没回来）可知，敲门的人'不可能'是王老师。can't表示'不可能'，用于否定推测。A选项must表示'一定是'，与句意相反；B选项might表示'可能是'，语气不确定，但此处是确定不可能；D选项should表示'应该'，不表示推测。解题技巧：must表肯定推测'一定是'，can't表否定推测'不可能是'，might/may表不确定推测'可能是'。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The task is too difficult. I think it is ______ for a 10-year-old child to finish it alone.
questions.explanation: 正确答案是B。根据前句'too difficult'（太难了）可知，对10岁孩子来说'不可能'独自完成。impossible意为'不可能的'。A选项possible（可能的）与句意相反；C选项necessary（必要的）和D选项important（重要的）与difficult的语境不符。解题技巧：利用上下文逻辑关系，前句说困难，后句应选负面词汇；注意im-前缀表否定。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: If you add 'un-' to the word 'happy', you get 'unhappy', which means feeling sad. Similarly, 'healthy' becomes '______' meaning not in good health.
questions.explanation: 正确答案是A。前缀un-表示'不、非'，加在形容词前构成反义词，如happy-unhappy, healthy-unhealthy。B选项re-表示'再次'；C选项dis-虽然也表示否定，但通常不与healthy搭配；D选项im-通常加在以b/m/p开头的形容词前（如impossible）。解题技巧：常见否定前缀：un-（最常见）、dis-（如dislike）、im-（如impossible）、in-（如incorrect）、ir-（如irregular）。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: We need to _______ a good plan to protect the environment in our school.
questions.explanation: 正确答案是A。come up with意为'想出、提出（主意、计划等）'，与plan搭配恰当。B选项look forward to意为'期待'，后接名词或doing，不与plan搭配表示'制定计划'；C选项get on with意为'与...相处'或'继续做'；D选项look up to意为'尊敬、敬仰'。解题技巧：come up with = think up（想出），重点记忆与plan/idea/solution的搭配。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.explanation: 正确答案是A。根据上下文，故事发生在last month（上个月），整体时态为一般过去时，此处描述老师'给我们做演讲'，用give的过去式gave。B选项是一般现在时第三人称单数；C是现在进行时；D是现在完成时，均与全文时态不符。解题技巧：完形填空首先通读全文把握时态背景，本文讲述过去的事件，主体时态为一般过去时。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a new project called "Green Campus". The goal was to make our school more environmentally friendly. All students were encouraged to take part in this meaningful activity.

On the first day, our teacher Mr. Li (8)______ us a talk about the importance of protecting the environment. He told us that small actions could make a big difference. Then, we were divided into groups to work on different tasks.

My group was responsible for (9)______ the school garden. We found that there was too much rubbish around the trees. It took us two hours to clean it all up. Although we felt tired, we were happy to see the garden become clean and tidy (10)______.

Another group set up several recycling boxes in each classroom. They also made posters to teach students how to sort waste (11)______. By the end of the week, everyone in our school became more aware of environmental protection.

This project has not only improved our school environment but also taught us the value of teamwork. We hope to (12)______ such activities in the future to make our world a better place.

questions.explanation: 正确答案是B。be responsible for是固定搭配，意为'对...负责'，其中for是介词，后面接名词或动名词（doing形式）。cleaning是clean的动名词形式。A选项是动词原形；C选项介词后不能接to do；D选项是过去式或过去分词。解题技巧：介词（in/on/at/for/of等）后面接动词时，要用动名词形式（doing）。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a new project called "Green Campus". The goal was to make our school more environmentally friendly. All students were encouraged to take part in this meaningful activity.

On the first day, our teacher Mr. Li (8)______ us a talk about the importance of protecting the environment. He told us that small actions could make a big difference. Then, we were divided into groups to work on different tasks.

My group was responsible for (9)______ the school garden. We found that there was too much rubbish around the trees. It took us two hours to clean it all up. Although we felt tired, we were happy to see the garden become clean and tidy (10)______.

Another group set up several recycling boxes in each classroom. They also made posters to teach students how to sort waste (11)______. By the end of the week, everyone in our school became more aware of environmental protection.

This project has not only improved our school environment but also taught us the value of teamwork. We hope to (12)______ such activities in the future to make our world a better place.

questions.explanation: 正确答案是A。again意为'又、再'，表示花园'再次'变得干净整洁（之前是脏的，打扫后恢复了干净）。B选项too（也）通常用于句末，但意思不符；C选项either用于否定句句末；D选项also（也）用于句中。解题技巧：根据上下文逻辑，此处强调'恢复原状'，用again最贴切。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a new project called "Green Campus". The goal was to make our school more environmentally friendly. All students were encouraged to take part in this meaningful activity.

On the first day, our teacher Mr. Li (8)______ us a talk about the importance of protecting the environment. He told us that small actions could make a big difference. Then, we were divided into groups to work on different tasks.

My group was responsible for (9)______ the school garden. We found that there was too much rubbish around the trees. It took us two hours to clean it all up. Although we felt tired, we were happy to see the garden become clean and tidy (10)______.

Another group set up several recycling boxes in each classroom. They also made posters to teach students how to sort waste (11)______. By the end of the week, everyone in our school became more aware of environmental protection.

This project has not only improved our school environment but also taught us the value of teamwork. We hope to (12)______ such activities in the future to make our world a better place.

questions.explanation: 正确答案是B。此处需要副词修饰动词sort（分类），表示'正确地分类垃圾'。correctly是correct的副词形式。A和C是形容词，不能修饰动词；D选项incorrectly（错误地）与句意相反。解题技巧：修饰动词要用副词，一般在形容词后加-ly构成副词；根据上下文，制作海报是为了教学生'正确'分类，而非错误分类。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a new project called "Green Campus". The goal was to make our school more environmentally friendly. All students were encouraged to take part in this meaningful activity.

On the first day, our teacher Mr. Li (8)______ us a talk about the importance of protecting the environment. He told us that small actions could make a big difference. Then, we were divided into groups to work on different tasks.

My group was responsible for (9)______ the school garden. We found that there was too much rubbish around the trees. It took us two hours to clean it all up. Although we felt tired, we were happy to see the garden become clean and tidy (10)______.

Another group set up several recycling boxes in each classroom. They also made posters to teach students how to sort waste (11)______. By the end of the week, everyone in our school became more aware of environmental protection.

This project has not only improved our school environment but also taught us the value of teamwork. We hope to (12)______ such activities in the future to make our world a better place.

questions.explanation: 正确答案是A。continue意为'继续'，符合句意'我们希望将来继续这样的活动'。B选项stop（停止）、C选项forget（忘记）、D选项refuse（拒绝）均与上下文表达的积极态度不符。前文提到活动不仅改善了环境还教会了团队合作价值，所以应该是希望'继续'。解题技巧：根据文章情感线索，结尾通常是升华主题，表达希望持续或推广，选积极词汇。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a new project called "Green Campus". The goal was to make our school more environmentally friendly. All students were encouraged to take part in this meaningful activity.

On the first day, our teacher Mr. Li (8)______ us a talk about the importance of protecting the environment. He told us that small actions could make a big difference. Then, we were divided into groups to work on different tasks.

My group was responsible for (9)______ the school garden. We found that there was too much rubbish around the trees. It took us two hours to clean it all up. Although we felt tired, we were happy to see the garden become clean and tidy (10)______.

Another group set up several recycling boxes in each classroom. They also made posters to teach students how to sort waste (11)______. By the end of the week, everyone in our school became more aware of environmental protection.

This project has not only improved our school environment but also taught us the value of teamwork. We hope to (12)______ such activities in the future to make our world a better place.

questions.stem: According to the passage, how many hours of sleep do teenagers need every night?
questions.explanation: 正确答案是C。根据文章第一段第二句'Studies show that teenagers need about 8 to 10 hours of sleep every night.'可知，青少年每晚需要8-10小时睡眠。这是直接细节题，在原文中可直接找到答案。A、B选项数量不足，D选项过多。解题技巧：细节理解题采用'关键词定位法'，在题干中找出teenagers/hours等关键词，快速定位到原文第一段查找具体数字。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解
questions.group_passage:
Sleep is very important for middle school students. Studies show that teenagers need about 8 to 10 hours of sleep every night. However, many students don't get enough sleep because of heavy homework and the use of electronic devices.

Scientists have found that sleep helps the brain process information learned during the day. When you sleep, your brain is actually working hard to store memories and solve problems. This is why you sometimes wake up with a solution to a difficult math problem.

Lack of sleep can cause many problems. It not only affects your concentration in class but also harms your health. Students who don't sleep well often feel sleepy during the day and may have trouble remembering new knowledge.

To sleep better, experts suggest creating a bedtime routine. This means going to bed at the same time every night and keeping electronic devices out of the bedroom. Regular exercise during the day can also help you fall asleep more easily, but avoid doing sports right before bedtime.

Remember, good sleep habits are the foundation of good learning. By improving your sleep quality, you will find yourself more energetic and efficient in your studies.

questions.stem: What can we learn from the passage about electronic devices?
options: A. They help students finish homework faster.
B. They should be used right before sleeping.
C. They are one reason why students lack sleep.
D. They can improve memory when used at night.
questions.explanation: 正确答案是C。根据第一段最后一句'many students don't get enough sleep because of heavy homework and the use of electronic devices'可知，电子设备是学生睡眠不足的原因之一。A选项文中未提及；B选项与第四段'keeping electronic devices out of the bedroom'（让电子设备远离卧室）相悖；D选项文中未提及且与科学常识不符。解题技巧：推理判断题要基于原文信息合理推断，不能主观臆断，也不能选择原文明确否定的选项。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断
questions.group_passage:
Sleep is very important for middle school students. Studies show that teenagers need about 8 to 10 hours of sleep every night. However, many students don't get enough sleep because of heavy homework and the use of electronic devices.

Scientists have found that sleep helps the brain process information learned during the day. When you sleep, your brain is actually working hard to store memories and solve problems. This is why you sometimes wake up with a solution to a difficult math problem.

Lack of sleep can cause many problems. It not only affects your concentration in class but also harms your health. Students who don't sleep well often feel sleepy during the day and may have trouble remembering new knowledge.

To sleep better, experts suggest creating a bedtime routine. This means going to bed at the same time every night and keeping electronic devices out of the bedroom. Regular exercise during the day can also help you fall asleep more easily, but avoid doing sports right before bedtime.

Remember, good sleep habits are the foundation of good learning. By improving your sleep quality, you will find yourself more energetic and efficient in your studies.

questions.stem: The underlined word 'concentration' in Paragraph 3 probably means ______.
options: A. the ability to remember things
B. the ability to focus attention
C. the ability to create new ideas
D. the ability to solve problems
questions.explanation: 正确答案是B。根据第三段上下文'Lack of sleep can cause many problems. It not only affects your concentration in class...'（睡眠不足会影响你在课堂上的concentration），结合常识，睡眠不足会导致上课走神、无法集中注意力，所以concentration意为'注意力'。A是memory（记忆力），C是creativity（创造力），D是problem-solving ability（解决问题的能力）。解题技巧：词义猜测题利用上下文语境，看同一句或前后句的解释说明；con-前缀表示'共同'，centr-词根表示'中心'，字面意思'把思想集中在中心'即'专注'。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测
questions.group_passage:
Sleep is very important for middle school students. Studies show that teenagers need about 8 to 10 hours of sleep every night. However, many students don't get enough sleep because of heavy homework and the use of electronic devices.

Scientists have found that sleep helps the brain process information learned during the day. When you sleep, your brain is actually working hard to store memories and solve problems. This is why you sometimes wake up with a solution to a difficult math problem.

Lack of sleep can cause many problems. It not only affects your concentration in class but also harms your health. Students who don't sleep well often feel sleepy during the day and may have trouble remembering new knowledge.

To sleep better, experts suggest creating a bedtime routine. This means going to bed at the same time every night and keeping electronic devices out of the bedroom. Regular exercise during the day can also help you fall asleep more easily, but avoid doing sports right before bedtime.

Remember, good sleep habits are the foundation of good learning. By improving your sleep quality, you will find yourself more energetic and efficient in your studies.

questions.stem: What is the best title for this passage?
options: A. How to Do Homework Efficiently
B. The Dangers of Electronic Devices
C. Why Sleep Matters for Students
D. Ways to Exercise Before Bedtime
questions.explanation: 正确答案是C。文章第一段讲睡眠对青少年的重要性，第二段讲睡眠帮助大脑处理信息，第三段讲睡眠不足的危害，第四段讲如何改善睡眠，最后总结良好睡眠是良好学习的基础。全文围绕'睡眠对学生的重要性'展开。A选项只涉及作业效率，片面；B选项只涉及电子设备，是细节而非主旨；D选项只涉及运动，是细节。解题技巧：主旨大意题采用'首尾段+各段首句'法，看文章高频词（sleep出现多次），标题要概括全文而非局部细节。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意
questions.group_passage:
Sleep is very important for middle school students. Studies show that teenagers need about 8 to 10 hours of sleep every night. However, many students don't get enough sleep because of heavy homework and the use of electronic devices.

Scientists have found that sleep helps the brain process information learned during the day. When you sleep, your brain is actually working hard to store memories and solve problems. This is why you sometimes wake up with a solution to a difficult math problem.

Lack of sleep can cause many problems. It not only affects your concentration in class but also harms your health. Students who don't sleep well often feel sleepy during the day and may have trouble remembering new knowledge.

To sleep better, experts suggest creating a bedtime routine. This means going to bed at the same time every night and keeping electronic devices out of the bedroom. Regular exercise during the day can also help you fall asleep more easily, but avoid doing sports right before bedtime.

Remember, good sleep habits are the foundation of good learning. By improving your sleep quality, you will find yourself more energetic and efficient in your studies.

options: A. However, I kept listening and tried to follow the pronunciation.
B. In addition, I began reading English books for thirty minutes every day.
C. As a result, I failed my English exam last semester.
D. Then, I decided to give up learning English completely.
E. As a result, my English grade improved from 60 to 85 in one term.
questions.explanation: 正确答案是C。前句提到'My English grades were poor, and I was afraid to speak in class'（我英语成绩差，不敢在课堂上发言），C选项'结果，我上学期英语考试不及格'是前文情况导致的自然结果，As a result（结果是）表示因果关系。A选项However表转折，但后文没有转折含义；B选项In addition表递进，但此处是总结结果而非添加信息；D选项决定放弃与后文'Then I decided to make some changes'（然后我决定改变）逻辑矛盾；E选项是成绩提高，与前文'成绩差'矛盾。解题技巧：选句子填空要看前后逻辑关系，此处是前因后果，用As a result连接。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students find learning English difficult. I used to be one of them. My English grades were poor, and I was afraid to speak in class. (17)______ Then I decided to make some changes.

First, I started listening to English songs every morning. At first, I couldn't understand the lyrics at all. (18)______ I looked up new words in the dictionary and wrote them down in a notebook. Slowly, I began to recognize more words.

(19)______ Every weekend, I spent an hour reading simple English stories. At first, I read very slowly, but I didn't give up. After three months, I noticed that I could read much faster than before.

These methods really worked for me. (20)______ Now I am no longer afraid of English classes, and I even enjoy answering questions in English. If you are struggling with English, why not try these tips?

options: A. However, I kept listening and tried to follow the pronunciation.
B. In addition, I began reading English books for thirty minutes every day.
C. As a result, I failed my English exam last semester.
D. Then, I decided to give up learning English completely.
E. As a result, my English grade improved from 60 to 85 in one term.
questions.explanation: 正确答案是A。前句说'起初我根本听不懂歌词'，A选项'然而，我继续听并试着跟读'形成转折关系（However），表示虽然听不懂但坚持不放弃，与后文'查字典、记单词'的努力过程衔接自然。B选项In addition表递进，但此处需要转折；C选项As a result表结果，但听不懂不会直接导致考试失败（且C已在17题使用）；D选项放弃与后文努力相悖；E选项是最终结果，应放在最后。解题技巧：看到'At first... couldn't understand'，后面通常接转折词However/But，表示'虽然...但是...'的对比结构。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students find learning English difficult. I used to be one of them. My English grades were poor, and I was afraid to speak in class. (17)______ Then I decided to make some changes.

First, I started listening to English songs every morning. At first, I couldn't understand the lyrics at all. (18)______ I looked up new words in the dictionary and wrote them down in a notebook. Slowly, I began to recognize more words.

(19)______ Every weekend, I spent an hour reading simple English stories. At first, I read very slowly, but I didn't give up. After three months, I noticed that I could read much faster than before.

These methods really worked for me. (20)______ Now I am no longer afraid of English classes, and I even enjoy answering questions in English. If you are struggling with English, why not try these tips?

options: A. However, I kept listening and tried to follow the pronunciation.
B. In addition, I began reading English books for thirty minutes every day.
C. As a result, I failed my English exam last semester.
D. Then, I decided to give up learning English completely.
E. As a result, my English grade improved from 60 to 85 in one term.
questions.explanation: 正确答案是B。前文讲了第一个方法（听英文歌），此处开始讲第二个方法（读英文故事），B选项'此外，我开始每天读30分钟英语书'用In addition（此外、另外）表示递进关系，引出另一个学习方法。A选项However表转折，但此处是添加新方法；C选项As a result表结果，但此处是列举方法；D选项放弃与文意相反；E选项是总结结果，不应放在中间。解题技巧：看到前文有First（第一），后面通常有In addition/Besides/Also（此外）引出第二、第三个方法，形成列举关系。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students find learning English difficult. I used to be one of them. My English grades were poor, and I was afraid to speak in class. (17)______ Then I decided to make some changes.

First, I started listening to English songs every morning. At first, I couldn't understand the lyrics at all. (18)______ I looked up new words in the dictionary and wrote them down in a notebook. Slowly, I began to recognize more words.

(19)______ Every weekend, I spent an hour reading simple English stories. At first, I read very slowly, but I didn't give up. After three months, I noticed that I could read much faster than before.

These methods really worked for me. (20)______ Now I am no longer afraid of English classes, and I even enjoy answering questions in English. If you are struggling with English, why not try these tips?

options: A. However, I kept listening and tried to follow the pronunciation.
B. In addition, I began reading English books for thirty minutes every day.
C. As a result, I failed my English exam last semester.
D. Then, I decided to give up learning English completely.
E. As a result, my English grade improved from 60 to 85 in one term.
questions.explanation: 正确答案是E。前句'These methods really worked for me'（这些方法对我很有效），E选项'结果，我的英语成绩从60分提高到了85分'用As a result引出具体成效，与前文构成因果关系，且与后文'现在不再害怕英语课'衔接自然。A、B选项是方法，不是结果；C选项是负面结果，与worked（有效）矛盾；D选项放弃与文意相反。解题技巧：前句出现worked/helped/succeeded等表示'有效'的词时，后句通常用As a result/Therefore引出积极结果或具体数据证明。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students find learning English difficult. I used to be one of them. My English grades were poor, and I was afraid to speak in class. (17)______ Then I decided to make some changes.

First, I started listening to English songs every morning. At first, I couldn't understand the lyrics at all. (18)______ I looked up new words in the dictionary and wrote them down in a notebook. Slowly, I began to recognize more words.

(19)______ Every weekend, I spent an hour reading simple English stories. At first, I read very slowly, but I didn't give up. After three months, I noticed that I could read much faster than before.

These methods really worked for me. (20)______ Now I am no longer afraid of English classes, and I even enjoy answering questions in English. If you are struggling with English, why not try these tips?
